name: Siahkal Incident (1971)
description: On 8 February 1971, a small armed group affiliated with the nascent [Organization of Iranian People's Fadai Guerrillas](/orgs/organization-of-iranian-peoples-fadai-guerrillas) attacked a gendarmerie post in the village of Siahkal in the forested mountains of Gilan province. The operation, intended partly to free a captured comrade, was militarily modest but symbolically transformative. The Shah's regime responded with a massive manhunt that killed or captured most of the participants, and thirteen guerrillas were subsequently executed. Despite its failure as a military operation, the Siahkal incident became the founding myth of the Iranian armed left, celebrated as proof that organised resistance to the Pahlavi monarchy was possible. [Massoud Ahmadzadeh](/people/massoud-ahmadzadeh) and [Amir Parviz Pouyan](/people/amir-parviz-pouyan) were among the theorists whose ideas animated the operation, while [Hamid Ashraf](/people/hamid-ashraf) was among those who survived the immediate aftermath. The date — 19 Bahman in the Iranian calendar — became an annual commemoration for the Iranian left in exile. The incident directly triggered the formation of the OIPFG as a consolidated organisation.
editorial_notes: Founding event of the Fadaiyan. Feb 8 / 19 Bahman 1971.
